{"covered_dims": {"R1.2": 0.7, "R1.5": 0.8, "R1.6": 0.9}}

Cleaned up the conclusion: tightened prose, regrouped paragraphs by topic (findings → mechanism → limitations → future work), and removed redundancy.

---

This paper quantifies a substantial commitment collapse in constrained reinforcement learning for V2G fleet aggregation across three European markets. Under rolling re-planning, none of the RL variants achieves market-viable commitment: soft penalties (PPO) reach ~45%, Lagrangian relaxation with a static horizon (CMDP) ~50%, and horizon-matched retraining (CMDP rolling-trained) 0%. By contrast, a model-based strategy that embeds inter-window coupling directly in its LP objective sustains commitment above 99.6%.

Supplementary experiments isolate the mechanism. Post-hoc coupling applied to RL restores commitment to 100% but eliminates arbitrage revenue, indicating that the coupling constraint must enter the planning objective rather than be patched onto its outputs. Our comparison spans pure RL, an engineered model-based stack, and two hybrid coupling schemes (post-hoc and integrated), with the integrated form realized inside the LP optimizer.

Several limitations bound these conclusions. Historical day-ahead prices are replayed, but fleet dynamics remain simulated, so absolute cost figures should be read as relative rather than deployment-grade projections. PPO's commitment weight and CMDP's learning rate were not exhaustively tuned, although the observed gradient dominance suggests soft-penalty remedies are structurally limited rather than a tuning artifact. Battery degradation is not modeled, and heterogeneous fleets would alter the constraint landscape.

Whether end-to-end RL architectures can internalize inter-window coupling as a differentiable constraint while preserving arbitrage capability remains open, and is the natural direction for future work.

---

Changes made: moved the headline quantitative result and the model-based contrast into the opening paragraph; consolidated the mechanism finding (post-hoc vs. integrated coupling) into one paragraph; grouped all limitations together; ended with a single forward-looking sentence rather than splitting it across the opening and closing. Removed the redundant sentence stating rolling-protocol training was evaluated on all three markets (already implied by the headline result).

No factual claims were altered; if the ~45% / ~50% / 99.6% figures or the three-market scope are stated differently elsewhere in the paper, reconcile accordingly.
